contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्री हनुमत् कवचस्तोत्रमहामन्त्रस्य वसिष्ठ ऋषिः अनुष्टुप् छन्दः श्री हनुमान् देवता मारुतात्मज इति बीजं अञ्जनासूनुरिति शक्तिः वायुपुत्र इति कीलकं हनुमत्प्रसाद सिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrī hanumat kavachastōtramahāmantrasya vasiṣṭha ṛṣiḥ anuṣṭup Chandaḥ śrī hanumān dēvatā mārutātmaja iti bījaṃ añjanāsūnuriti śaktiḥ vāyuputra iti kīlakaṃ hanumatprasāda siddhyarthē japē viniyōgaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dedication (Viniyoga): Of this Śrī Hanumat Kavaca Stotram Mahāmantra: the seer is Vasiṣṭha; the metre is Anuṣṭup; the deity is Śrī Hanumān; the seed-syllable is "Mārutātmaja"; the power is "Añjanāsūnu"; the pin is "Vāyuputra". Its application is declared for the attainment of Hanumān's grace.

contentBlocks.original:
उल्लङ्घ्य सिन्धोस्सलिलं सलीलं
यश्शोकवह्निं जनकात्मजायाः ।
आदाय तेनैव ददाह लङ्कां
नमामि तं प्राञ्जलिराञ्जनेयम् ॥ 1
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ullaṅghya sindhōssalilaṃ salīlaṃ
yaśśōkavahniṃ janakātmajāyāḥ ।
ādāya tēnaiva dadāha laṅkāṃ
namāmi taṃ prāñjalirāñjanēyam ॥ 1
contentBlocks.translation: I bow with folded hands to Āñjanēya (Hanumān) — who leaped playfully across the ocean's waters, who took the fire of Jānakī's (Sītā's) sorrow and with that very fire burned Laṅkā itself. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
मनोजवं मारुततुल्यवेगं
जितेन्द्रियं बुद्धिमतां वरिष्ठम् ।
वातात्मजं वानरयूथमुख्यं
श्रीरामदूतं शिरसा नमामि ॥ 2
contentBlocks.transliteration:
manōjavaṃ mārutatulyavēgaṃ
jitēndriyaṃ buddhimatāṃ variṣṭham ।
vātātmajaṃ vānarayūthamukhyaṃ
śrīrāmadūtaṃ śirasā namāmi ॥ 2
contentBlocks.translation: Swift as thought, equal in speed to the wind, conqueror of the senses, foremost among the wise — born of the Wind, chief of the monkey host, the divine messenger of Śrī Rāma — to him I bow with my head. (2)
Like the rising sun in brilliance, of noble strength of arms, beautiful as ten million Kāmadevas, expert in all branches of knowledge — (3)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामहृदयानन्दं भक्तकल्पमहीरुहम् ।
अभयं वरदं दोर्भ्यां कलये मारुतात्मजम् ॥ 4
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmahṛdayānandaṃ bhaktakalpamahīruham ।
abhayaṃ varadaṃ dōrbhyāṃ kalayē mārutātmajam ॥ 4
contentBlocks.translation: — the delight of Śrī Rāma's heart, the wish-fulfilling Kalpa tree for devotees, one who bestows fearlessness and boons with both arms — I meditate on the son of Mārutas. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीराम राम रामेति रमे रामे मनोरमे ।
सहस्रनाम तत्तुल्यं रामनाम वरानने ॥ 5
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāma rāma rāmēti ramē rāmē manōramē ।
sahasranāma tattulyaṃ rāmanāma varānanē ॥ 5
contentBlocks.translation: "Śrī Rāma Rāma Rāma" — I delight in Rāma, in the mind-enchanting Rāma. The name "Rāma" is equal to a thousand (divine) names — such is the greatness of Rāma's name, O fair-faced one. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
पादौ वायुसुतः पातु रामदूतस्तदङ्गुलीः ।
गुल्फौ हरीश्वरः पातु जङ्घे चार्णवलङ्घनः ॥ 6
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pādau vāyusutaḥ pātu rāmadūtastadaṅguḻīḥ ।
gulphau harīśvaraḥ pātu jaṅghē chārṇavalaṅghanaḥ ॥ 6
contentBlocks.translation: May Vāyusuta (son of the Wind) protect my feet; may Rāmadūta (messenger of Rāma) protect my toes. May Harīśvara (lord of Hari's monkeys) protect my ankles; may Arṇavalaṅghana (the ocean-leaper) protect my calves. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
जानुनी मारुतिः पातु ऊरू पात्वसुरान्तकः ।
गुह्यं वज्रतनुः पातु जघनं तु जगद्धितः ॥ 7
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jānunī mārutiḥ pātu ūrū pātvasurāntakaḥ ।
guhyaṃ vajratanuḥ pātu jaghanaṃ tu jagaddhitaḥ ॥ 7
contentBlocks.translation: May Mārutī protect my knees; may Asurāntaka (destroyer of demons) protect my thighs. May Vajratanu (of diamond body) protect my private parts; may Jagaddhita (benefactor of the world) protect my hips. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
आञ्जनेयः कटिं पातु नाभिं सौमित्रिजीवनः ।
उदरं पातु हृद्गेही हृदयं च महाबलः ॥ 8
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āñjanēyaḥ kaṭiṃ pātu nābhiṃ saumitrijīvanaḥ ।
udaraṃ pātu hṛdgēhī hṛdayaṃ cha mahābalaḥ ॥ 8
contentBlocks.translation: May Āñjanēya protect my waist; may Saumitrijīvana (giver of life to Lakṣmaṇa) protect my navel. May Hṛdgēhī (dweller in the heart) protect my abdomen; may Mahābala (the supremely powerful) protect my heart. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
वक्षो वालायुधः पातु स्तनौ चाऽमितविक्रमः ।
पार्श्वौ जितेन्द्रियः पातु बाहू सुग्रीवमन्त्रकृत् ॥ 9
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vakṣō vālāyudhaḥ pātu stanau chā'mitavikramaḥ ।
pārśvau jitēndriyaḥ pātu bāhū sugrīvamantrakṛt ॥ 9
contentBlocks.translation: May Vālāyudha (one whose weapon is his tail) protect my chest; may Amitavikrama (of immeasurable valor) protect my breast. May Jitēndriya (conqueror of the senses) protect my sides; may Sugrīvamantrakṛt (counselor of Sugrīva) protect my arms. (9)

contentBlocks.original:
करावक्ष जयी पातु हनुमांश्च तदङ्गुलीः ।
पृष्ठं भविष्यद्र्बह्मा च स्कन्धौ मति मतां वरः ॥ 10
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karāvakṣa jayī pātu hanumāṃścha tadaṅguḻīḥ ।
pṛṣṭhaṃ bhaviṣyadrbahmā cha skandhau mati matāṃ varaḥ ॥ 10
contentBlocks.translation: May Akṣajayī (victor over Akṣa) protect my hands; may Hanumān protect my fingers. May Bhaviṣyadbrahmā (the future Brahmā) protect my back; may Mati-matām-vara (best among the wise) protect my shoulders. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
कण्ठं पातु कपिश्रेष्ठो मुखं रावणदर्पहा ।
वक्त्रं च वक्तृप्रवणो नेत्रे देवगणस्तुतः ॥ 11
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṇṭhaṃ pātu kapiśrēṣṭhō mukhaṃ rāvaṇadarpahā ।
vaktraṃ cha vaktṛpravaṇō nētrē dēvagaṇastutaḥ ॥ 11
contentBlocks.translation: May Kapiśrēṣṭha (foremost among monkeys) protect my throat; may Rāvaṇadarpahā (destroyer of Rāvaṇa's pride) protect my face. May Vaktṛpravaṇa (gifted with eloquent speech) protect my mouth; may Dēvagaṇastuta (praised by the host of gods) protect my eyes. (11)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मास्त्रसन्मानकरो भ्रुवौ मे पातु सर्वदा ।
कामरूपः कपोले मे फालं वज्रनखोऽवतु ॥ 12
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmāstrasanmānakarō bhruvau mē pātu sarvadā ।
kāmarūpaḥ kapōlē mē phālaṃ vajranakhō'vatu ॥ 12
contentBlocks.translation: May the one who accepted the Brahmāstra with equanimity protect my eyebrows always. May Kāmarūpa (one who assumes any form at will) protect my cheeks; may Vajranakha (of thunderbolt claws) protect my forehead. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
शिरो मे पातु सततं जानकीशोकनाशनः ।
श्रीरामभक्तप्रवरः पातु सर्वकलेबरम् ॥ 13
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śirō mē pātu satataṃ jānakīśōkanāśanaḥ ।
śrīrāmabhaktapravaraḥ pātu sarvakaḻēbaram ॥ 13
contentBlocks.translation: May Jānakīśōkanāśana (destroyer of Jānakī's sorrow) always protect my head. May Śrīrāmabhaktapravara (chief among devotees of Śrī Rāma) protect my entire body. (13)

contentBlocks.original:
मामह्नि पातु सर्वज्ञः पातु रात्रौ महायशाः ।
विवस्वदन्तेवासी च सन्ध्ययोः पातु सर्वदा ॥ 14
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māmahni pātu sarvajñaḥ pātu rātrau mahāyaśāḥ ।
vivasvadantēvāsī cha sandhyayōḥ pātu sarvadā ॥ 14
contentBlocks.translation: May Sarvajña (the all-knowing) protect me by day; may Mahāyaśas (of great renown) protect me by night. May the disciple of the Sun (trained by Sūrya himself) protect me at both twilights, always. (14)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मादिदेवतादत्तवरः पातु निरन्तरम् ।
य इदं कवचं नित्यं पठेच्च शृणुयान्नरः ॥ 15
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmādidēvatādattavaraḥ pātu nirantaram ।
ya idaṃ kavachaṃ nityaṃ paṭhēchcha śṛṇuyānnaraḥ ॥ 15
contentBlocks.translation: May the one who received boons from Brahmā and the gods protect me without cease. The person who daily recites and listens to this kavacham — (15)

contentBlocks.original:
दीर्घमायुरवाप्नोति बलं दृष्टिं च विन्दति ।
पादाक्रान्ता भविष्यन्ति पठतस्तस्य शत्रवः ।
स्थिरां सुकीर्तिमारोग्यं लभते शाश्वतं सुखम् ॥ 16
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dīrghamāyuravāpnōti balaṃ dṛṣṭiṃ cha vindati ।
pādākrāntā bhaviṣyanti paṭhatastasya śatravaḥ ।
sthirāṃ sukīrtimārōgyaṃ labhatē śāśvataṃ sukham ॥ 16
contentBlocks.translation: — attains a long life, gains strength and clarity of vision. The enemies of the one who recites it shall be crushed underfoot; one obtains enduring good reputation, health, and eternal happiness. (16)

contentBlocks.original:
इति निगदितवाक्यवृत्त तुभ्यं
सकलमपि स्वयमाञ्जनेय वृत्तम् ।
अपि निजजनरक्षणैकदीक्षो
वशग तदीय महामनुप्रभावः ॥ 17
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti nigaditavākyavṛtta tubhyaṃ
sakalamapi svayamāñjanēya vṛttam ।
api nijajanarakṣaṇaikadīkṣō
vaśaga tadīya mahāmanuprabhāvaḥ ॥ 17
contentBlocks.translation: O Āñjanēya — all your own deeds have now been spoken in these verse-clusters, for you. O one devoted solely to the protection of your own people — the power of the great mantra of Rāma is ever at your command. (17)
Thus ends the Śrī Hanumat Kavacham.
